schema_json.title: StormAnswer

prompt:
You are a research expert being interviewed about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting?

The interviewer (Mark Haub, Industry perspective) asks:
QUESTION: What do published effectiveness, adherence, and retention data from commercial intermittent-fasting mobile applications (Zero, Fastic, LIFE Fasting Tracker, Simple, DoFasting) actually show for real-world user outcomes over 12-24 weeks, and how do these real-world effect sizes compare to the weight-loss magnitudes reported in peer-reviewed RCTs that the apps cite in their marketing?

SEARCH RESULTS (your ONLY source of information):
[Source 1] Retention, Fasting Patterns, and Weight Loss With an Intermittent ...
  URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9579929/
  Content: This study aimed to quantify user retention, fasting patterns, and weight loss by users of 2 IF mobile apps.
[Source 2] Retention, Fasting Patterns, and Weight Loss With an Intermittent ...
  URL: https://mhealth.jmir.org/2022/10/e35896
  Content: In our study of mobile app users in the real-world setting, we found that IF is an effective strategy for weight loss for many people.
[Source 3] The effectiveness of mobile app usage in facilitating weight loss - PMC
  URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11091450/
  Content: This study demonstrates the effectiveness of engaging with the SIMPLE mobile application in achieving WL in adults with overweight and obesity in a real‐world ...
[Source 4] (PDF) Retention, Fasting Patterns, and Weight Loss With an ...
  URL: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/362290711_Retention_Fasting_Patterns_and_Weight_Loss_With_an_Intermittent_Fasting_App_A_Large-scale_52-week_Observational_Study_Preprint
  Content: By week 26, users lost over 1% of their starting weight per EFH per day on average. The regression analysis using all variables was highly ...
[Source 5] Your First 3 Months with Zero: What to Expect
  URL: https://zerolongevity.com/blog/your-first-3-months-with-zero-what-to-expect/
  Content: After 3 Months with Zero, you'll have lost more weight (particularly fat!) and improved numerous markers of metabolic health.
[Source 6] Zero: Fasting & Food Tracker - App Store
  URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/zero-fasting-food-tracker/id1168348542
  Content: Zero is the all-in-one fasting tracker and nutrition app built for real results. Most fasting and weight loss apps stop at simply tracking fasts or calories.
[Source 7] Time-Restricted Eating in Real-World Healthcare Settings - MDPI

  Content: The modest but significant reductions in weight, BMI, and waist circumference observed among patients advised to follow TRE in our studied clinic align with ...
[Source 8] Zero: Fasting & Food Tracker - Overview - Apple App Store - US
  URL: https://app.sensortower.com/overview/1168348542?country=US
  Content: Zero is the all-in-one fasting tracker and nutrition app built for real results. Most fasting and weight loss apps stop at simply tracking fasts or calories.
[Source 9] Is Zero Fasting App Worth $70? The 2025 Reality Check No One's ...
  URL: https://medium.com/@ed_55112/is-zero-fasting-app-worth-70-the-2025-reality-check-no-ones-talking-about-35145cb991c6
  Content: Multiple documented cases of 30+ pound weight loss in 2.5 months; Users achieving 50–100 pound transformations through consistent usage ...

  URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002916522010322
  Content: Intermittent fasting (IF) and Paleolithic (Paleo) diets produce weight loss in controlled trials, but minimal evidence exists regarding long-term efficacy under ...
[Source 11] Retention, Fasting Patterns, and Weight Loss With an ... - PMC
  URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9579929/
  Content: by L Torres · 2022 · Cited by 18 — This study aimed to quantify user retention, fasting patterns, and weight loss by users of 2 IF mobile apps.
[Source 12] The effectiveness of mobile app usage in facilitating weight loss
  URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11091450/
  Content: by R Huntriss · 2024 · Cited by 3 — The study demonstrates that the SIMPLE app enables effective WL directly associated with the level of app engagement.

  URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/org/science/article/pii/S2561326X23007047
  Content: by S Valinskas · 2023 · Cited by 22 — Mobile apps can act as platforms that help deliver dietary interventions by improving adherence and motivation. Although the effect of IF on weight loss has ...

  URL: https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04836312
  Content: This pilot study will randomize 34 patients with hypertension and obesity to either time-restricted feeding alone or a commitment device to encourage ...
[Source 15] Fasting and weight loss: mobile application-based approach
  URL: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s40795-022-00645-1
  Content: by S Valinskas · 2022 · Cited by 6 — A randomized controlled trial has demonstrated that adherence to a dietary weight loss intervention is significantly higher when delivered ...

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Provide a comprehensive, well-structured answer (200-400 words).
2. ONLY use information from the search results above. Do NOT add information not present in the sources.
3. When citing specific facts, mention which source they come from.
4. Extract 3-5 key findings as bullet points.
5. List the URLs of sources you actually used in your answer.
6. If the search results do not adequately answer the question, say so explicitly and note what information is missing.

Return a JSON object with 'answer', 'key_findings', and 'sources_used' fields.
